title: PCP Event
description: The canonical change wrapper inside PCP. Every PcpMessage body is exactly one Event describing a change to one entity. PCP communicates project CHANGES, not raw records. An Event carries at least one of: a full 'entity' snapshot, a 'delta', or 'refs'.

entity_type.description: The kind of entity this event concerns. Closed set in v1 (the eight canonical nouns).

verb.description: The lifecycle verb: an assertion of recorded change, NOT a transition authorization. Open string with a controlled vocabulary (created, updated, status_changed, snapshot, deleted, plus entity-specific verbs documented in SPEC.md). Consumers MUST tolerate-and-pass unknown verbs.

entity_id.description: Id of the affected entity. Self-asserted: PCP gives NO referential-integrity guarantee and ids are NOT guaranteed globally unique across independent producers. Scoping is the consumer's responsibility via transport context, not via this field.

project_id.description: Optional, untrusted scope hint indicating the project the entity belongs to.

occurred_at.description: When the change actually happened (as opposed to PcpMessage.issued_at = when the message was composed).

attributed_to.description: Free-form opaque provenance label of who/what is said to have made the change. UNTRUSTED, non-authoritative, NOT an identity assertion, grants nothing. MUST NOT carry a 'scheme:value' identity structure.

entity.description: Optional full snapshot of the affected entity. When present, it is validated against the schema for 'entity_type'.

delta.description: Optional change set: a map of field name to { from?, to }. Leaf values are scalar or null; changes to array/object fields are communicated via a snapshot instead. A delta is a CLAIM about change, not a transaction log: no ordering or causality is guaranteed between deltas.

refs.description: Optional semantic links to related entities or external references.
